mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of month-over-month PAYEMS changes versus 20-day S&P 500 returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 1.0% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and declining unemployment establishes a self-reinforcing demand loop that lifts corporate top-line growth and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal from RSAFS and UNRATE fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with walk-forward Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in unemployment during late-cycle expansions reflects deteriorating labor market momentum that precedes downward earnings revisions and risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: In late-cycle regimes, triggered UNRATE increases do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side output gaps that reduce aggregate corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly declines in INDPRO are not followed by significantly negative 20-day S&P 500 returns compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1000 thousand units validates durable residential investment demand and construction-related employment spillovers that boost cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1000, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in the consumer price index outrunning the nominal federal funds rate creates negative real interest rates that incentivize leveraged corporate investment and reflate equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The spread between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS fails to generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate output and income generation that supports broad-based earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 do not correlate with positive 60-day forward S&P 500 returns with Spearman correlation above 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.
